perturbation.original_text: Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) Between Central Denmark Region Business reg.-no. 29190925 Skottenborg 26 DK-8800 Viborg (hereinafter referred to as ”Central Denmark Regio n”) and Name: ____________________________ Business reg. no. _________________ Address: __________________________ Country: ___________________________ (hereinafter referred to as “the Tenderer”) (each a "Party" and together the "Parties") this Non-Disclosure Agreement has been entered into in connection with "Udbud, hardware til præhospitale køretøjer (ambulancer, akutlægebiler m v.)".
perturbation.changed_text: Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) Between Central Denmark Region Business reg.-no. 29190925 Skottenborg 26 DK-8800 Viborg (hereinafter referred to as ”Central Denmark Regio n”) and Name: ____________________________ Business reg. no. _________________ Address: __________________________ Country: ___________________________ (hereinafter referred to as “the Applicant”) (each a "Party" and together the "Parties") this Non-Disclosure Agreement has been entered into in connection with "Udbud, hardware til præhospitale køretøjer (ambulancer, akutlægebiler m v.)".
perturbation.explanation: Changing "the Tenderer" to "the Applicant" introduces ambiguity. While both might be interpreted as the other party, there's no explicit definition stating they are the same. This is a deliberate terminology shift that, without proper context, raises questions about the actual parties involved in the agreement. This makes unclear for a given scenario, the obligations apply to 'the Tenderer', 'the Applicant' or both.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) Between Central Denmark Region Business reg.-no. 29190925 Skottenborg 26 DK-8800 Viborg (hereinafter referred to as ”Central Denmark Regio n”) and Name: ____________________________ Business reg. no. _________________ Address: __________________________ Country: ___________________________ (hereinafter referred to as “the Applicant”) (each a "Party" and together the "Parties") this Non-Disclosure Agreement has been entered into in connection with "Udbud, hardware til præhospitale køretøjer (ambulancer, akutlægebiler m v.)".
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the second party as "the Tenderer", which is used in other parts of the contract. The changed text redefines the second party as "the Applicant". The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning that if obligations apply to 'the Tenderer', it's no longer clear whether the obligations apply to 'the Applicant' or both, creating ambiguity and therefore a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: However, Confidential Information shall not include such information which (i) is or has become public ly known other than through a breach of a confidential ity obligation, (ii) has been received from an independent third party without any restrictions or obligation of confidentiality, or (iii) which the receiving Party can demonstrate has been independently develo ped by that Party prior to disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: However, Protected Information shall not include such information which (i) is or has become public ly known other than through a breach of a confidential ity obligation, (ii) has been received from an independent third party without any restrictions or obligation of confidentiality, or (iii) which the receiving Party can demonstrate has been independently develo ped by that Party prior to disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Confidential Information" with "Protected Information" but keeping the rest of the agreement referencing "Confidential Information" creates a conflict. The exceptions to the definition now apply to 'Protected Information,' but the obligations refer to 'Confidential Information.' It is now unclear, on what scenarios this exception applies to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Parties’ making available t o each other Confidential Information as defined in Sub- clause 1.1, each Party hereby agrees to the followi ng confidentiality undertakings for and on behalf of itself and for and on behalf of the Party’s employees, adv isors and representatives: • All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Confidential Information" with "Protected Information" in the exception clause, while the contradicted text references "Confidential Information" specifically. This creates a direct conflict because the exceptions now apply to a different term than the obligations. The obligations are tied to 'Confidential Information', but the exceptions define what is excluded from 'Protected Information'. This creates a strong contradiction, making it unclear when the confidentiality undertakings apply, because it is unclear which term they are supposed to consider.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Parties’ making available t o each other Confidential Information as defined in Sub- clause 1.1, each Party hereby agrees to the followi ng confidentiality undertakings for and on behalf of itself and for and on behalf of the Party’s employees, adv isors and representatives: • All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Parties’ making available t o each other Classified Information as defined in Sub- clause 1.1, each Party hereby agrees to the followi ng confidentiality undertakings for and on behalf of itself and for and on behalf of the Party’s employees, adv isors and representatives: • All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the initial reference to "Confidential Information" to "Classified Information" in this section, while subsequent clauses still refer to "Confidential Information," introduces a direct conflict. It is unclear whether the obligations relate to Classified Information, Confidential Information, or both. This impacts the enforceability of the confidentiality clauses, the definition is changing in one of the initial mentions of the world, so the agreement itself is contradicting itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Parties’ making available t o each other Classified Information as defined in Sub- clause 1.1, each Party hereby agrees to the followi ng confidentiality undertakings for and on behalf of itself and for and on behalf of the Party’s employees, adv isors and representatives: • All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Classified Information' in the initial consideration clause. However, the subsequent clauses within the same section still refer to 'Confidential Information'. This introduces a contradiction because it is unclear whether the confidentiality undertakings apply to Classified Information, Confidential Information, or both. This creates ambiguity and directly contradicts the original intention of the clause, impacting its enforceability. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall have in effect and maintain adequ ate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall have in effect and maintain adequ ate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Data from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" without clarifying if they are the same, creates uncertainty. If 'Confidential Data' is broader than 'Confidential Information', the scope of security measures expands without defining the specifics. It is unclear what that means, if the security measures need to expand or no, the parties can say 'that data wasn't agreed'
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, title and interest (including without li mitation all intellectual property and other propri etary rights) throughout the world in and to any and all Confiden tial Information disclosed by a Party as well as inventions, discoveries, improvements, ideas, conce pts, designs, know-how, data, code, software, produ cts, 3 information, documentation, techniques, materials a nd/or other work product in any form and on whatever media and any enhancements, upgrades, modi fications and/or alterations thereto in any form, created, conceived, prepared, made, developed, orig inated and/or delivered (in whole or in part) by or for a Party shall belong exclusively and absolutely to such Party, its sub-contractors and/or licensors (a s the case may be).
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information,' while the changed text uses 'Confidential Data.' The perturbation explanation states this creates uncertainty, particularly if 'Confidential Data' is broader than 'Confidential Information,' expanding the scope of security measures without definition. The contradicted text defines rights relating to 'Confidential Information.' If 'Confidential Data' now includes items not covered by 'Confidential Information', then the provision in the Contradicted Text is unclear in its scope and thus, it is now in contradiction to the Changed Text. The change introduces ambiguity and potentially expands the scope of the agreement beyond what was originally intended, thus there is a strong in-text contradiction.
